# Review: `deepmind/1006.lean` â€” ErdÅ‘s Problem 1006

## 1. Copyright Header

- [x] File begins with the **exact** copyright block (no leading blank line).
- [x] Year is exactly `2026`.

## 2. Import Statement

- [x] Exactly one import line, immediately after the copyright block (separated by one blank line): `import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports`.
- [x] No other imports are present.

## 3. Module Docstring

- [x] A `/-! ... -/` module docstring appears after the import, separated by one blank line.
- [x] Starts with a heading: `# ErdÅ‘s Problem 1006`.
- [x] Contains a `*Reference:*` line with a markdown link to the source.
- [ ] **FAIL:** Includes a brief plain-English description of the problem. The module docstring only contains the title, reference link, and bibliography entries. The actual problem description appears in the theorem docstring (line 64â€“73) but is absent from the module docstring itself.
- [x] Optional bibliography entries present: `[Er71]`, `[Er76b]`, `[NeRo78b]`.

## 4. Namespace

- [x] All declarations are inside `namespace Erdos1006 ... end Erdos1006`.
- [x] Namespace name uses `UpperCamelCase`: `Erdos1006`.
- [x] `open SimpleGraph` appears **before** the namespace block (line 32).

## 5. Custom Definitions

- [x] `Orientation` structure has a docstring (`/-- ... -/`, line 38â€“40).
- [x] `Orientation.IsAcyclic` def has a docstring (line 47â€“48).
- [x] `flipDir` def has a docstring (line 52â€“53).
- [x] `Orientation.IsRobustlyAcyclic` def has a docstring (line 57â€“58).
- [x] No `noncomputable` needed (no classical choice or nonconstructive operations).
- [x] No `sorry` in definitions.
- [x] Types/Props use `UpperCamelCase`: `Orientation`, `IsAcyclic`, `IsRobustlyAcyclic`.
- [x] Function returning a type uses `lowerCamelCase`: `flipDir`.
- N/A: No local notation used (none needed).
- N/A: `@[category test]`/`@[category API]` sanity checks (optional).

## 6. Theorem Statements

- [x] Main theorem uses the `theorem` keyword.
- [x] Named `erdos_1006` (snake_case, matching the namespace number).
- [x] Name follows Lean/Mathlib conventions.
- [x] Has both `@[category ...]` and `@[AMS ...]` on the **same attribute line**: `@[category research solved, AMS 5]`.
- [x] Main theorem has a docstring (lines 64â€“73).
- [x] Docstring references source papers: `[Er71]`, `[Er76b]`, `[NeRo78b]`.

### `answer()` usage

The problem asks a yes/no question: *"Can the edges of G always be directed such that..."* â€” **Case 1** applies.

- [x] Theorem type is `answer(False) â†” P`, correctly using Case 1 form.
- [x] `answer(False)` is used (not `answer(sorry)`) since the problem has been disproved.
- [x] The RHS formalizes the question as originally asked: `âˆ€ (V : Type*) (G : SimpleGraph V), 5 â‰¤ G.egirth â†’ âˆƒ o : Orientation G, o.IsRobustlyAcyclic`. "Yes" (`True`) would mean every such graph admits a robustly acyclic orientation; "No" (`False`) means it does not â€” matching the disproof by NeÅ¡etÅ™il and RÃ¶dl.
- [x] Quantifiers appear **after** `answer(False) â†”`, not as theorem parameters.

## 7. Category Attribute

- [x] Exactly one `@[category ...]` attribute present.
- [x] Value is `research solved` â€” valid (disproved by NeÅ¡etÅ™il and RÃ¶dl but not formally verified in Lean).
- N/A: `research open` sorry-rule does not apply.

## 8. AMS Attribute

- [x] Exactly one `@[AMS 5]` attribute present.
- [x] At least one AMS subject number listed: `5` (Combinatorics).
- [x] `5` is a valid AMS MSC2020 code.
- N/A: Only one number, so ordering/duplicate checks do not apply.

## 9. Proof Bodies

- [x] Proof uses `by` / `sorry` on separate lines (multi-line form, acceptable per checklist).
- [x] No `sorry` in `def`, `abbrev`, or `structure` declarations.
- [x] No informal proof sketches (problem is solved, not open).
- [x] No `native_decide`.

## 10. Variants & Multi-part Problems

- N/A: No variants present.

## 11. Code Quality

- [x] 2-space indentation throughout.
- [x] Unicode math symbols used where applicable: `âˆ€`, `âˆƒ`, `â†’`, `Â¬`, `â†”`.
- [x] No debug code (`#check`, `#eval`, `#print`, `dbg_trace`).
- [x] No `set_option` disabling linters.
- [x] Lines are reasonably short (all under 100 characters).
- [x] File ends with a single trailing newline.

## 12. Build Verification

- [ ] Not verified (requires `lake build`).

---

## Summary

**One issue found:**

1. **Â§3 Module Docstring â€” missing plain-English description.** The module docstring contains the title, reference link, and bibliography but no brief description of the problem. The problem description ("Let G be a graph with girth > 4...") currently lives only in the theorem docstring (lines 64â€“73). A short summary should be added to the module docstring.

**Overall:** Clean, well-structured file. The custom definitions (`Orientation`, `IsAcyclic`, `flipDir`, `IsRobustlyAcyclic`) are well-documented and correctly typed. The `answer(False) â†” ...` form correctly models the disproved yes/no question. The only fix needed is adding a plain-English problem summary to the module docstring.

    25â†’

    51â†’

    53â†’keeping all other directed edges the same. -/

/-
Copyright 2026 The Formal Conjectures Authors.

Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
You may obtain a copy of the License at

    https://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0

Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
limitations under the License.
-/

import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports

/-!
# ErdÅ‘s Problem 1006

*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/1006](https://www.erdosproblems.com/1006)

[Er71] ErdÅ‘s, P., *Some unsolved problems in graph theory and combinatorial analysis*, 1971.

[Er76b] ErdÅ‘s, P., *Problems and results in graph theory*, 1976.

[NeRo78b] NeÅ¡etÅ™il, J. and RÃ¶dl, V., *A short proof of the existence of highly chromatic
hypergraphs without short cycles*, 1978.
-/

open SimpleGraph

namespace Erdos1006

variable {V : Type*}

/-- An orientation of a simple graph $G$: a relation `dir` on $V$ such that for each
edge $\{u, v\}$, exactly one of `dir u v` or `dir v u` holds, and `dir` only
relates adjacent vertices. -/
structure Orientation (G : SimpleGraph V) where
  dir : V â†’ V â†’ Prop
  adj_of_dir : âˆ€ u v, dir u v â†’ G.Adj u v
  dir_of_adj : âˆ€ u v, G.Adj u v â†’ dir u v âˆ¨ dir v u
  antisymm : âˆ€ u v, dir u v â†’ Â¬dir v u

/-- An orientation is acyclic if the transitive closure of its direction relation
is irreflexive (equivalently, there are no directed cycles). -/
def Orientation.IsAcyclic {G : SimpleGraph V} (o : Orientation G) : Prop :=
  âˆ€ v, Â¬Relation.TransGen o.dir v v

/-- The relation obtained by flipping one directed edge $(a \to b)$ to $(b \to a)$,
keeping all other directed edges the same. -/
def flipDir (dir : V â†’ V â†’ Prop) (a b : V) : V â†’ V â†’ Prop :=
  fun u v => (u = b âˆ§ v = a âˆ§ dir a b) âˆ¨ (dir u v âˆ§ Â¬(u = a âˆ§ v = b))

/-- An orientation is robustly acyclic if it is acyclic and reversing any
single directed edge also yields an acyclic relation. -/
def Orientation.IsRobustlyAcyclic {G : SimpleGraph V} (o : Orientation G) : Prop :=
  o.IsAcyclic âˆ§
  âˆ€ a b, o.dir a b â†’
    âˆ€ v, Â¬Relation.TransGen (flipDir o.dir a b) v v

/--
ErdÅ‘s Problem 1006 (disproved) [Er71][Er76b]:

Let $G$ be a graph with girth $> 4$ (i.e., $G$ contains no cycles of length $3$ or $4$).
Can the edges of $G$ always be directed such that there is no directed cycle, and
reversing the direction of any edge also creates no directed cycle?

This was disproved by NeÅ¡etÅ™il and RÃ¶dl [NeRo78b], who proved that for every
integer $g$, there is a graph $G$ with girth $g$ such that every orientation of $G$ either
contains a directed cycle or contains a cycle obtained by reversing one edge.
-/
@[category research solved, AMS 5]
theorem erdos_1006 : answer(False) â†”
    âˆ€ (V : Type*) (G : SimpleGraph V), 5 â‰¤ G.egirth â†’
    âˆƒ o : Orientation G, o.IsRobustlyAcyclic := by
  sorry

end Erdos1006

input.new_string:
*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/1006](https://www.erdosproblems.com/1006)

Let $G$ be a graph with girth $> 4$. Can the edges of $G$ always be oriented so that the
resulting directed graph is acyclic, and reversing any single edge also leaves it acyclic?
Disproved by NeÅ¡etÅ™il and RÃ¶dl.

[Er71]

import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports

/-!
# ErdÅ‘s Problem 1006

*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/1006](https://www.erdosproblems.com/1006)

[Er71] ErdÅ‘s, P., *Some unsolved problems in graph theory and combinatorial analysis*, 1971.

[Er76b] ErdÅ‘s, P., *Problems and results in graph theory*, 1976.

[NeRo78b] NeÅ¡etÅ™il, J. and RÃ¶dl, V., *A short proof of the existence of highly chromatic
hypergraphs without short cycles*, 1978.
-/

open SimpleGraph

namespace Erdos1006

variable {V : Type*}

/-- An orientation of a simple graph $G$: a relation `dir` on $V$ such that for each
edge $\{u, v\}$, exactly one of `dir u v` or `dir v u` holds, and `dir` only
relates adjacent vertices. -/
structure Orientation (G : SimpleGraph V) where
  dir : V â†’ V â†’ Prop
  adj_of_dir : âˆ€ u v, dir u v â†’ G.Adj u v
  dir_of_adj : âˆ€ u v, G.Adj u v â†’ dir u v âˆ¨ dir v u
  antisymm : âˆ€ u v, dir u v â†’ Â¬dir v u

/-- An orientation is acyclic if the transitive closure of its direction relation
is irreflexive (equivalently, there are no directed cycles). -/
def Orientation.IsAcyclic {G : SimpleGraph V} (o : Orientation G) : Prop :=
  âˆ€ v, Â¬Relation.TransGen o.dir v v

/-- The relation obtained by flipping one directed edge $(a \to b)$ to $(b \to a)$,
keeping all other directed edges the same. -/
def flipDir (dir : V â†’ V â†’ Prop) (a b : V) : V â†’ V â†’ Prop :=
  fun u v => (u = b âˆ§ v = a âˆ§ dir a b) âˆ¨ (dir u v âˆ§ Â¬(u = a âˆ§ v = b))

/-- An orientation is robustly acyclic if it is acyclic and reversing any
single directed edge also yields an acyclic relation. -/
def Orientation.IsRobustlyAcyclic {G : SimpleGraph V} (o : Orientation G) : Prop :=
  o.IsAcyclic âˆ§
  âˆ€ a b, o.dir a b â†’
    âˆ€ v, Â¬Relation.TransGen (flipDir o.dir a b) v v

/--
ErdÅ‘s Problem 1006 (disproved) [Er71][Er76b]:

Let $G$ be a graph with girth $> 4$ (i.e., $G$ contains no cycles of length $3$ or $4$).
Can the edges of $G$ always be directed such that there is no directed cycle, and
reversing the direction of any edge also creates no directed cycle?

This was disproved by NeÅ¡etÅ™il and RÃ¶dl [NeRo78b], who proved that for every
integer $g$, there is a graph $G$ with girth $g$ such that every orientation of $G$ either
contains a directed cycle or contains a cycle obtained by reversing one edge.
-/

lines: +Let $G$ be a graph with girth $> 4$. Can the edges of $G$ always be oriented so that the
+resulting directed graph is acyclic, and reversing any single edge also leaves it acyclic?
